description_long: This street cabinet consists of three rectangular metal enclosures mounted on a sturdy metal frame, with two stacked vertically on one side and a third offset to the right. The front-facing boxes feature analog dials, warning labels, and visible wiring, suggesting electrical or utility control functions. Several thick black cables run from the bottom of the enclosures down to the base, secured by the frame. The surfaces are painted a muted gray-blue, with some weathering and minor rust visible on the supporting frame. One of the enclosures has a vented panel, likely for heat dissipation, and another displays a warning triangle and text label. The overall construction appears robust and designed for outdoor urban environments, such as sidewalks or utility areas.
description: A robust, gray-blue metal street cabinet with three enclosures, analog dials, warning labels, and exposed wiring on a weathered metal frame.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked arrangement of the two main enclosures and the protruding cables.
description_view_1: Front view displaying analog dials, warning labels, and visible wiring on the enclosures.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the vented panel and the offset third enclosure.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain backs of the enclosures and the bundled cables running down the frame.
